rationale: X directly engages the one substantive GT concern (yenuo26's reduce_scatter/device_communicator assertions) by quoting and validating those exact lines, while Y never mentions it, only offering a generic 'test coverage: PASS.' X's extra findings (missing docstring on the new init_vllm_model_parallel_group, monkeypatch-only test coverage of the real vLLM path, undocumented blocking-collective assumption) are grounded in the diff and give precise file/line fixes, though the enable_expert_parallel docstring complaint is arguably out-of-scope/pre-existing, and 'major' severity is inflated given the PR was actually approved. Y's review is technically deeper and better calibrated to the true LGTM outcome (it correctly reasons through DPMetadata construction and cleanup semantics), but it's less actionable — mostly narrative pass/fail table plus one comment — and misses the one concrete concern reviewers actually raised.
